purposes.description: Retrieve current weather conditions for a given location using wttr.in via curl

primitives.evidence: Execute a single curl command against wttr.in, e.g. curl "wttr.in/London?format=3" (Commands > Current Weather)
Construct a curl command with the correct URL and query parameters for the target city (Commands > Current Weather)
Must include a city/region/airport code in the query as stated in the Location section

purposes.description: Retrieve multi-day weather forecasts for a given location using wttr.in via curl

primitives.evidence: Execute curl commands for 3-day or week forecasts, e.g. curl "wttr.in/London?format=v2" (Commands > Forecasts)
Construct curl command with day-selection query parameter ?0, ?1, ?2 or format=v2 (Commands > Forecasts)
Must include a location identifier in the query (Location section)

purposes.description: Customize weather output format using wttr.in format codes for specific fields like temperature, wind, humidity

primitives.evidence: Build format strings with codes like %c, %t, %f, %w, %h, %p, %l (Format Options and Format Codes sections)
Execute the formatted curl request and use its output (Quick Responses section)
Select appropriate output format: one-liner, JSON (j1), or PNG based on user need (Format Options section)

purposes.description: Interpret the curl output and present a natural language weather summary to the user

primitives.evidence: Compose a short natural language answer from the curl output for questions like 'What's the weather?' or 'Will it rain?' (Quick Responses section)
Parse and interpret the weather data returned by curl to extract relevant details (implicit in all usage patterns)
